meaning: adj.装满空气的,有气胎的,汽力的,风力的,灵魂的n.气胎
examples: The L/G support the aircraft on the ground and oil-pneumatic shock absorbers absorb taxi and landing loads.
I learned something: a pneumatic nail gun does not a carpenter make.
The sound of a snore (up to 69 decibels) can be almost as loud as the noise of a pneumatic drill.
Previous attempts to create such a robot have used gears and air-powered or pneumatic pumps.
In 1845, the first pneumatic tires were patented by British civil engineer Robert Thompson.
